name: openapi-spec-generation
description: Generate and maintain OpenAPI 3.1 specifications from code, design-first specs, and validation patterns. Use when creating API documentation, generating SDKs, or ensuring API contract compliance.
capabilities: Generate complete OpenAPI 3.1 specifications from scratch
Create specs using code-first approach with FastAPI or Express/tsoa
Validate API implementations against OpenAPI contracts
Set up API documentation portals with Redocly
Generate client SDKs in TypeScript, Python, and Go
Apply linting rules with Spectral for spec quality
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides instructional documentation for OpenAPI specification generation. All static findings are false positives: shell command examples are documentation samples, hardcoded URLs are example domains (example.com, localhost), JWT references are standard API authentication definitions, and there is no malicious code execution. The skill is safe for marketplace publication.
valueStatement: Create comprehensive OpenAPI 3.1 specifications for REST APIs using design-first, code-first, or hybrid approaches. Includes templates, validation patterns, and SDK generation guidance.
limitations: Does not execute or run API servers
Does not generate server implementation code directly
Requires user to provide API details and endpoints
Does not validate live API responses against specs

useCases.targetUser: API architects and backend developers building new services
useCases.title: New API Design
useCases.description: Create OpenAPI specs for new APIs using design-first approach with comprehensive examples for authentication, pagination, and error handling.

useCases.title: Existing API Documentation
useCases.description: Generate documentation from existing codebases using FastAPI or Express with tsoa annotations.

useCases.title: SDK Generation Workflow
useCases.description: Generate client SDKs in multiple languages from OpenAPI specs for frontend and external consumers.

promptTemplates.title: Create Basic API Spec
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an OpenAPI 3.1 specification for a [RESOURCE] API with CRUD operations. Include authentication via Bearer token, pagination support, and standard error responses.

promptTemplates.title: Add Authentication Security
promptTemplates.prompt: Add JWT Bearer authentication and API key security schemes to an existing OpenAPI spec. Include 401 and 403 error responses.

promptTemplates.title: Generate Python Client SDK
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a Python client SDK from this OpenAPI specification. Include authentication handling and proper error types.

promptTemplates.title: Validate and Lint Spec
promptTemplates.prompt: Create Spectral linting rules for this OpenAPI spec that enforce operation IDs, require descriptions, and validate security definitions.

bestPractices: Use $ref to reuse schemas, parameters, and responses across the spec
Include real-world examples in the spec to help API consumers
Define all possible error codes and include descriptive messages
Version your API in the URL path and use semantic versioning for spec changes
antiPatterns: Avoid generic descriptions like 'Gets a resource' - be specific about what is returned
Do not skip security definitions - every endpoint should have authentication documented
Do not mix naming conventions - stay consistent with camelCase or snake_case throughout

faq.question: What is the difference between design-first and code-first?
faq.answer: Design-first writes the OpenAPI spec before writing code. Code-first generates the spec from code annotations. Design-first is better for new APIs and contracts. Code-first is better for existing codebases.

faq.question: Which languages are supported for code-first generation?
faq.answer: The skill includes examples for Python with FastAPI and TypeScript with Express/tsoa. Other frameworks like Spring Boot, Rails, and NestJS have their own OpenAPI generation tools.

faq.question: How do I validate my OpenAPI spec?
faq.answer: Use Spectral for linting with custom rules. Use Redocly for bundle validation. Both can be run from command line and integrated into CI/CD pipelines.

faq.question: Can this skill generate server code?
faq.answer: No - this skill focuses on specification creation and validation. Use OpenAPI Generator for generating server stubs and client SDKs from specs.

faq.question: What authentication schemes are supported?
faq.answer: OpenAPI supports API Key, Bearer/JWT, OAuth 2.0, and HTTP authentication. The skill includes examples for Bearer token and API Key schemes.

faq.question: How do I version my API spec?
faq.answer: Common approaches include URL versioning (/v1/, /v2/), header versioning, or query parameter versioning. The spec version in the info object tracks spec changes.
